name: Rent Increase
description: The landlord pushed your housing cost up and your margin shrank overnight.
log_entry: Rent went up again, and the month got tighter before it even started.

name: Roommate Conflict
description: Bills, noise, resentment, and bad timing all landed at once.
log_entry: Your place turned ugly and the month paid for it.

name: Reduced Hours / Layoff
description: Your income lane took a hit and the plan immediately got shakier.
log_entry: Work dried up hard this month.

name: Promotion Window
description: A small opening appeared above you. If your momentum is real, it might matter.

choices.label: Push for scope
choices.description: Take the stretch assignment, chase the visibility, and accept the pressure that comes with it.

choices.label: Bank consistency
choices.description: Keep the month steadier, protect your energy, and turn the opening into a slower credibility win.

log_entry: A promotion window opened if you can capitalize on it.

name: Car Repair
description: One bad sound became one expensive month.
log_entry: Your vehicle demanded money you did not want to spend.

name: Used Car Window
description: A decent mechanic says there is a narrow chance to upgrade smarter than usual.

choices.label: Take the deal
choices.description: Commit to the upgrade and accept the extra pressure.

choices.label: Walk away
choices.description: Keep your current setup and protect your margin.

log_entry: A rare decent used-car deal is on the table for a short time.

name: Scholarship / Help Grant
description: You caught a break that lowers the cost of staying on your education path.
log_entry: You landed help money right when you needed it.

name: Family Emergency
description: Someone close to you needed help and your own month took the hit.

choices.label: Cover it
choices.description: Send money and absorb the hit to your own runway.

choices.label: Set boundary
choices.description: Protect your own month and let the tension settle.

log_entry: Family trouble pulled money and focus out of your month.

name: Burnout Month
description: You kept pushing and your body finally made you feel it.
log_entry: Your body finally called the bluff.

name: Transit Hike
description: Transit got more expensive again and the monthly math shifted under you.
log_entry: Transit fares jumped again.

name: Apprenticeship Opening
description: A rare opening appeared for someone willing to commit to the trades lane.
log_entry: Someone tipped you off to an apprenticeship opening while it was still real.

name: Economic Downturn
description: The whole month gets tighter. Nobody is as generous with hours or opportunity as they were.
log_entry: The wider economy softened and the whole month felt it.

name: Side Hustle Window
description: A clean little money window opened if you can actually take advantage of it.
log_entry: A short side-hustle window put quick money on the table.

name: Parent Tension Spike
description: Staying home stopped feeling like support and started feeling like conflict.
log_entry: Home stopped feeling like free stability and started feeling like pressure.

name: Social Isolation Slide
description: You kept grinding and the rest of your life started thinning out.
log_entry: The month felt emptier, and your performance started paying for it.

name: Market Correction Shock
description: The market corrected hard this month and punished aggressive exposure.
log_entry: Risk assets took a hit and your aggressive bucket felt it.

name: Roommate Missed the Bill
description: Your roommate came up short and your own month absorbed the shock.
log_entry: A roommate missed their share and your month got uglier fast.

name: Roommate Moves Out
description: The place got quieter, but the rent split got a lot worse.
log_entry: Your roommate bailed, and suddenly the cheap place stopped being cheap.

name: Parent Bailout
description: Family stepped in, but it cost some pride and some future goodwill.
log_entry: Family covered part of the mess, but it used up some of the buffer between you.

name: Parent Boundary
description: The message was clear: staying home without momentum is no longer free.
log_entry: Home is still cheaper than rent, but it no longer feels like unconditional support.

name: Beater Breakdown
description: The cheap car reminded you why it was cheap.
log_entry: The beater broke down and turned access into a problem instead of a benefit.

name: Missed Shift After Breakdown
description: The transport mess spilled into actual lost work.
log_entry: One breakdown turned into a missed shift and a worse month than planned.

name: Financed Car Insurance Spike
description: The financed car kept the access, but the monthly trap just got meaner.
log_entry: Insurance jumped, and now the financed car feels a lot less clever.

name: Credit Limit Review
description: The credit bureau notice makes it clear that your borrowing story is under review.

choices.label: Tighten up
choices.description: Trim the balance and protect your credit story.

choices.label: Coast
choices.description: Protect cash now and accept the credit drag.

log_entry: The bureau review made the month feel a lot less optional.

name: Refinance Window
description: Strong credit cracked open a rare chance to clean up expensive debt terms.

choices.label: Refinance now
choices.description: Swap expensive debt terms for a cleaner monthly lane.

choices.label: Wait
choices.description: Keep your current terms and protect your breathing room.

log_entry: Your credit opened a rare chance to make debt less ugly.

name: Credit Rebuild Window
description: A cleaner few months finally made a real credit-repair lane worth taking seriously.

choices.label: Open the secured line
choices.description: Pay a little now to let consistent behavior rebuild your file month by month.

choices.label: Keep repairing slowly
choices.description: Avoid the extra step and keep rebuilding through ordinary clean months.

log_entry: A legitimate credit repair lane opened because your month stopped looking chaotic.

name: Sales Hot Streak
description: Momentum finally showed up in public and this lane felt alive for once.
log_entry: Sales got hot and, for once, your lane felt like upside instead of pressure.

name: Sales Cold Streak
description: The same volatility that creates upside also creates ugly silent months.
log_entry: Sales went cold and reminded you how thin the floor is in this lane.

name: Vehicle Total Loss
description: The engine finally gave up for good. There is no fixing this one.
log_entry: The engine died. Your car is gone. You are back to transit or worse until you can replace it.

name: Inventory Crunch
description: Receiving missed the mark and the floor is short. The store is about to lean on whoever can absorb the chaos.

choices.label: Cover the floor yourself
choices.description: You save the month for the store, but your body pays for it.

choices.label: Protect the team
choices.description: You reset expectations, keep people from snapping, and give back some momentum.

log_entry: Operations pressure landed in your lap.

name: Big Ticket Month
description: A rare customer window opened. If you press it right, this month can pay differently.

choices.label: Push the close
choices.description: You squeeze the month for upside and take the pressure with it.

choices.label: Play the long game
choices.description: You bank the relationship instead of maxing the month.

log_entry: A sales-heavy month gave you a real choice between upside and stability.

name: Client Book Referral
description: A steady customer started feeding you repeat business and reputation instead of just random volume.
log_entry: Your relationship lane paid off this month.

name: Dock Bottleneck
description: A bad inbound wave jammed the floor. Leadership has to decide whether to brute-force it or protect the team.

choices.label: Stay late and clear it
choices.description: You save the dock but the month gets heavier.

choices.label: Run a safer shift
choices.description: You protect your people and accept a smaller career push.

log_entry: Operations stress hit the dock all at once.

name: Dispatch Route Rewrite
description: The route board is a mess. If you can untangle it, the office notices.

choices.label: Own the rollout
choices.description: You take point, earn visibility, and eat the coordination stress.

choices.label: Keep it local
choices.description: You fix your corner and stay steadier.

log_entry: Coordination work finally turned into a visible career moment.

name: Equipment Certification Window
description: A short training slot opened for higher-value equipment work. It costs money now to change the lane later.

choices.label: Pay for certification
choices.description: You take the near-term hit for a cleaner equipment path.

choices.label: Delay it
choices.description: You preserve the month, but the lane stays narrower for now.

log_entry: A specialized equipment route briefly opened.

name: Market Panic Window
description: The correction is forcing a choice: protect your nerves now or keep swinging for upside.

choices.label: Sell to safety
choices.description: You reduce pressure now, but it feels like conceding the run.

choices.label: Keep buying
choices.description: You lean into volatility and accept a shakier month.

log_entry: Your wealth stance stopped being abstract when the market turned.

name: Dry Powder Window
description: Because you kept more cushion, this correction can be a threat or a selective opportunity instead of just panic.

choices.label: Buy selectively
choices.description: Use your stability to press a measured edge.

choices.label: Protect the floor
choices.description: Keep the month calm and take the stability win.

log_entry: Liquidity turned a market scare into a real decision.

name: Retail Leadership Offer
description: Senior management is testing who can absorb more scope without losing the floor.

choices.label: Take closing command
choices.description: Take more store authority, move faster, and live with the extra pressure.

choices.label: Stabilize the floor
choices.description: Protect the team, bank credibility, and grow slower.

log_entry: Management scope is on the table if you want the pressure that comes with it.

name: Sales Territory Offer
description: A better territory opened. It can lift the lane or spike the volatility depending on how you take it.

choices.label: Go full commission
choices.description: Press the upside and accept that the lane gets more unstable.

choices.label: Take salary plus bonus
choices.description: Choose the steadier version of the offer and keep the run cleaner.

log_entry: A stronger territory opened and forced a real upside-versus-stability call.

name: Dispatch Lead Offer
description: The routing board needs a lead. Taking it changes whether you become the calm center or the bottleneck.

choices.label: Own the board
choices.description: Take control of dispatch flow and carry the new coordination stress.

choices.label: Stay coordination-side
choices.description: Keep the run steadier and let someone else wear the full pressure.

log_entry: Dispatch stopped being background work and turned into a real fork.

name: Equipment Shift Contract
description: Operations wants someone certified and reliable for the premium machine-heavy shifts.

choices.label: Sign the contract
choices.description: Take the pay bump and accept a harder body load.

choices.label: Keep standard hours
choices.description: Protect your body and stay on the lane without taking the heavier deal.

log_entry: Specialized equipment work finally offered real upside and real strain.

name: Equipment Specialist Offer
description: Your reliability record opened a specialist lane with better pay and heavier responsibility.

choices.label: Accept rotation
choices.description: Take the specialist lane now and absorb the heavier operating pressure.

choices.label: Stay current lane
choices.description: Keep the month steadier and decline the bigger specialist pressure.

log_entry: Reliable equipment work created a real upside-versus-pressure decision.

name: Equipment Safety Recall
description: A safety recall hit your lane during a high-pressure stretch and forced a hard call on output versus stability.

choices.label: Work through recall
choices.description: Push through the disruption to protect short-term output.

choices.label: Slow for safety
choices.description: Protect stability and reliability, accepting a short-term cash hit.

log_entry: Equipment pressure turned into a safety-versus-output choice.

name: Security Deposit Shock
description: Weak credit turned a normal housing move into an expensive screening and deposit problem.

choices.label: Pay the extra deposit
choices.description: Cover the credit penalty in cash and keep the housing lane open.

choices.label: Delay the move
choices.description: Protect cash now and accept another month of limitation.

log_entry: Credit fragility made a housing move more expensive than it looked.

name: Prime Vehicle Offer
description: Strong credit opened a financing lane with terms that are actually useful instead of predatory.

choices.label: Lock the offer
choices.description: Keep the financing window open so you can make the move on your terms.

choices.label: Skip it
choices.description: Keep your current transport plan and your monthly margin.

log_entry: Good credit finally opened a transport offer worth taking seriously.

name: Good Tenant Renewal
description: Strong credit and a clean solo-rental lane finally gave you leverage at renewal instead of just another bill.

choices.label: Lock in the renewal
choices.description: Take the cleaner lease terms and protect your monthly margin.

choices.label: Stay flexible
choices.description: Keep your options open instead of locking yourself in, even if the deal is decent.

log_entry: For once, good credit and a stable lease history put the renewal in your favor.

name: Beater Cascade
description: The beater is no longer one repair away from stable. You choose whether to patch it and pray or take the immediate hit to protect next month.

choices.label: Patch it and keep grinding
choices.description: Lower upfront pain, higher risk of missed work if it fails again.

choices.label: Eat the upgrade hit
choices.description: Take the bigger immediate cost to reduce near-term instability.

name: Overtime vs Exam Collision
description: Work pressure and school deadlines hit the same week. You can protect short-term cash or protect academic momentum.

choices.label: Protect grades
choices.description: Drop some shifts and preserve your education lane.

choices.label: Protect paycheck
choices.description: Take the income and absorb the academic drag.

log_entry: You had to choose which lane to disappoint this month.

name: Collections Warning
description: Missed payments and weak credit triggered a direct warning. You can pay now to hold the line or float it and risk a harsher follow-up.

choices.label: Stabilize now
choices.description: Cover the warning and protect your credit floor.

choices.label: Float it
choices.description: Keep cash now and risk a deeper credit hit next month.

name: Key Account Offer

choices.label: Keep it stable
choices.description: Lower upside, steadier social and stress profile.

description: Inconsistent follow-through is starting to cost repeat business. You can overcompensate now or accept a slower decline and protect your energy.

choices.label: Overrecover
choices.description: Win clients back this month at a heavy personal cost.

choices.label: Accept the dip

name: Foreman Offer

choices.label: Stay floor anchor

name: Warehouse Safety Crunch
description: Fatigue and floor pressure produced a safety scare. You can push through or pull back and absorb a short-term output hit.

choices.description: Keep output high but raise burnout risk.

choices.label: Slow and reset
choices.description: Protect the team and your body, lose some immediate momentum.

name: Deadline Overflow
description: The operations queue is stacking and leadership wants a same-week fix. You can brute-force the month or protect your runway.

choices.description: Preserve delivery metrics now and absorb a heavier personal hit.

choices.label: Stabilize delivery
choices.description: Protect team reliability and accept slower short-term progress.

name: Automation Rollout

choices.label: Stage rollout
choices.description: Roll out slower and preserve consistency.

name: Team Retention Wave

name: Compliance Backlog

choices.description: Stabilize controls now at a short-term fatigue cost.

choices.label: Trim scope

choices.label: Press output

name: Market Margin Call

choices.label: Hold the line
